data.body:
STORY 1 DONE: 79da (local signing) CLOSED. Materialized SSM key to ~/.ssh/rebar-bot-signing-key (0600), machine-wide ~/.config/rebar/config.toml [identity] signing_key. Probe ticket 4c55 signed+verified; all ACs proven. Force-closed (operational; verifier can't check runtime provisioning from a code tree; evidence recorded). NOTE: my ticket writes now SIGN.

REUSABLE PATTERNS learned:
1) Scoped-attestation: set file_impact (existing dep files) BEFORE review so the plan-review attestation is scoped -> claim survives tickets-branch advance (unscoped sshsig attestations hit the stale-head bug). For NON-code provisioning stories, REMOVE file_impact before close (else the close gate demands a referencing commit) then --force-close.
2) Proving 'a writer's new event is signed+verified': capture C=tickets HEAD before the write, then 'rebar verify-identity --all --require-authenticated --since C' exit 0 proves every event since C verifies (the real enforcement check). Do NOT filter verdict==verified in the JSON report (verified events are excluded from it), and do NOT use 'jq --arg u E' with a literal.

Now: 4dc0/1a51/8d91 re-reviewing with fixed --since proving commands.
